name: Balcón de Lobón Loop from Talavera la Real
name_original: Balcón de Extremadura desde Talavera la Real — circular
description: This moderate 31.4-mile mountain biking route from Talavera la Real offers scenic views of the Guadiana River and local reservoirs.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Talavera La Real traverse a landscape characterized by rolling terrain, open *dehesa* woodlands, and riverine paths. The region features ancient nature paths like the Cañada Real de las Bardocas, which offers diverse scenery for cyclists. Trails often follow the course of the Guadiana River and the Canal de Montijo, providing flat sections alongside water features. The area's topography generally presents moderate challenges, with routes suitable for riders with good fitness. Best mountain bike trails around Talavera La Real The most popular mountain bike trail is Talavera la Real and Badajoz Countryside Trail, a 28.0 miles (45.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the agricultural plains and open countryside between the two towns. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Talavera la Real to La Corte de Peleas, a moderate 32.8 miles (52.7 km) path. This route connects Talavera La Real with La Corte de Peleas, offering varied scenery through rural areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Balcón de Lobón Loop from Talavera la Real, a 31.4 miles (50.5 km) trail leading through open landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 41 minutes. Mountain biking around Talavera La Real is defined by *dehesa* landscapes, the Guadiana River, and the Canal de Montijo. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Talavera La Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 17 reviews. More than 188 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Talavera La Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Talavera La Real?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Talavera La Real, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The region is known for its moderate routes, with a few easier paths also available.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Talavera La Real?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Talavera La Real is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, so checking the forecast is always recommended.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on mountain bike trails in Talavera La Real?
A: You'll experience diverse landscapes, including rolling terrain, open &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; woodlands with their characteristic oak trees, and scenic paths along the Guadiana River and the Canal de Montijo. Ancient nature paths like the Cañada Real de las Bardocas also offer picturesque scenery.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Talavera La Real?
A: Most mountain bike trails in Talavera La Real are classified as moderate, suitable for riders with good fitness. There are also a few easier paths for those looking for a less challenging ride. The technical difficulty generally ranges from S0-S2 on the Single Trail Scale, meaning they vary from easy, flowing paths to sections requiring more advanced skills.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Talavera La Real?
A: While many routes are moderate, the region's flatter sections along the Canal de Montijo and the Guadiana River can be suitable for families looking for easier rides. Always check the route details for elevation and technical difficulty to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What attractions or points of interest can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Along the trails, you can encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5461924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de la Trampa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Montijo&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4216181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baden Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Peter the Apostle&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcony of Extremadura&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available from Talavera La Real?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358499362/balcon-de-lobon-loop-from-talavera-la-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Lobón Loop from Talavera la Real&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through open landscapes. Another is the 'Pueblonuevo del Guadiana y Talavera la Real – circular por la Cañada Real de las Bardocas desde Badajoz', which highlights the ancient Cañada Real.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Talavera La Real?
A: The mountain bike trails in Talavera La Real are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 17 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse scenery, the quiet countryside, and the well-maintained paths that allow for immersive outdoor exploration.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that follow the Canal de Montijo or Arroyo de la Trampa?
A: Yes, many routes in the region incorporate these features. The Canal de Montijo runs parallel to the Río Guadiana, offering flat sections and scenic views. The Arroyo de la Trampa is often encountered along the Cañada Real de las Bardocas, enhancing the natural beauty of the trails.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that connect Talavera La Real to other towns?
A: Absolutely. One notable route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358229988/talavera-la-real-to-la-corte-de-peleas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera la Real to La Corte de Peleas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate path offering varied scenery through rural areas. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357802437/talavera-la-real-and-badajoz-countryside-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera la Real and Badajoz Countryside Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the agricultural plains between the two towns.

Q: Is it possible to find parking near the mountain bike trailheads in Talavera La Real?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not always explicitly marked, Talavera La Real and its surrounding villages offer various public parking options. It's advisable to check local maps or use navigation apps to locate convenient parking near your chosen starting point.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in the area?
A: One of the longer routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdebotoa Bridge – Weir on the Guadiana River loop from Novelda del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 58.5 kilometers. This moderate trail offers an extensive ride through the region's landscapes.
tips: This small city of just over 5,000 inhabitants is located just a few kilometers from Badajoz. It is surrounded by agricultural fields and the Talavera la Real Air Base. With all the fields around, it is a good place to start a mountain bike ride to explore the area.
Balcón, which is located in the town of Lobón and from where you can contemplate the view of the Guadiana River passage. Nice place to be quiet and enjoy the views!

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Talavera La Real. You can typically find parking within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a 'moderate' mountain bike tour. It requires good basic fitness and sufficient technical skills, so it might be challenging for absolute beginners. Riders should be comfortable with longer stretches and potentially varied terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain can be varied, typical of Extremadura's landscape. You might encounter ancient paths through oak forests. While specific details for this route are not exhaustive, some trails in the region can feature complicated sections with steps and roots, so be prepared for diverse surfaces.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the scenic Extremaduran landscape. A notable highlight is the View of the Guadiana River from the Balcón de Lobón. The route also passes by the Embalse de Piedra Aguda, a weir on the Guadiana River, and the Lago de San Isidro, offering diverse natural scenery.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 50.5 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 40 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Balcón de Extremadura desde Talavera la Real' is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location, Talavera La Real.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Extremadura offers beautiful riding conditions, but consider the seasons. Spring and autumn generally provide milder temperatures, which are ideal for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is outdoors, specific rules for dogs are not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always carry water for your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to ride the 'Balcón de Extremadura desde Talavera la Real — circular' route.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino Natural del Guadiana, Camino de Las Loberas, and El Muro.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Extremadura is known as a 'biking and birding heaven' due to its well-preserved natural state. While specific sightings vary, you have a good chance of observing diverse bird species and other local wildlife in their natural habitat along the route.

waypoints.name: View of the Guadiana River from the Balcón de Lobón
